questions.questionText: A toy-car, blowing its horn, is moving with a steady speed of 5 m/s, away from a wall. An observer, towards whom the toy car is moving, is able to hear 5 beats per second. If the velocity of sound in air is 340 m/s, the frequency of the horn of the toy car is close to :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Let the frequency of the horn = f

Apparent frequency heared by the observer directly,

$${f_{dir}} = \left( {{V \over {V - {V_s}}}} \right)f = \left( {{{340} \over {340 - 5}}} \right)f = {{340} \over {335}}f$$

Apparent frequency heared by the observer on reflection from the wall,

$${f_{ind}} = \left( {{V \over {V + {V_s}}}} \right)f = \left( {{{340} \over {340 + 5}}} \right)f = {{340} \over {345}}f$$

Also, given that,

find $$-$$ fdir $$=$$ 5

$$ \Rightarrow $$   $${{340f} \over {345}}$$ $$-$$ $${{340f} \over {335}}$$ $$=$$ 5

$$ \Rightarrow $$   f $$=$$ $${5 \over {340}} \times {{335 \times 345} \over {10}}$$

= 169.9  $$ \simeq $$   170 Hz

questions.questionText:
In an experiment to determine the period of a simple pendulum of length 1 m, it is attached to different spherical bobs of radii r<sub>1</sub>
 and r<sub>2</sub> . The two spherical bobs have uniform mass distribution. If the relative difference in the periods, is found to be
5×10<sup>−4</sup>  s, the difference in radii, $$\left| {} \right.$$r<sub>1</sub> $$-$$ r<sub>2</sub> $$\left| {} \right.$$ is best given by :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
The time period is given by the formula

$$T = 2\pi \sqrt {{l \over g}} $$

which clearly indicates that the time period is directly proportional to the length of the pendulum l, that is,

$$T \propto \sqrt l $$

Here, l = 1 m. Therefore,

$${{\Delta T} \over l} = {1 \over 2}{{\Delta l} \over l}$$ ....... (1)

where $$\Delta l = \left| {{r_1} - {r_2}} \right|$$. Substituting the values in Eq. (1), we get

$$5 \times {10^{ - 4}} = {1 \over 2}\left( {{{{r_1} - {r_2}} \over 1}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {r_1} - {r_2} = 10 \times {10^{ - 4}} = {10^{ - 3}}$$ m $$ = {10^{ - 1}}$$ cm $$ = 0.1$$ cm

questions.questionText:
The motion of a mass on a spring, with spring constant K is as shown in figure.


The equation of motion is given by
x(t) = A sin$$\omega$$t + B cos$$\omega$$t with $$\omega$$ = $$\sqrt {{K \over m}} $$

Suppose that at time t = 0, the position of mass is x(0) and velocity v(0), then its displacement can also be represented as x(t) = C cos($$\omega$$t $$-$$ $$\phi$$), where C and $$\phi$$ are :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A closed organ pipe of length L and an open organ pipe contain gases of densities $$\rho$$<sub>1</sub> and $$\rho$$<sub>2</sub> respectively. The compressibility of gases are equal in both the pipes. Both the pipes are vibrating in their first overtone with same frequency. The length of the open pipe is $${x \over 3}L\sqrt {{{{\rho _1}} \over {{\rho _2}}}} $$ where x is ___________. (Round off to the Nearest Integer)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
First overtone of open pipe $$ = {{{v_2}} \over {{L_2}}}$$

First overtone of closed pipe at one end $$ = {{3v} \over {4L}}$$

As per question,

$${{3V} \over {4L}} = {{{V_2}} \over L}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \sqrt {{B \over {{\rho _1}}}} \,.\,{3 \over {4L}} = \sqrt {{B \over {{\rho _2}}}} \,.\,{1 \over {{L_2}}}$$ ($$\because$$ $$V = \sqrt {{B \over \rho }} $$)

$$ \Rightarrow {L_2} = {{4L} \over 3}\sqrt {{{{\rho _1}} \over {{\rho _2}}}} $$ ..... (i)

According to question, the length of the open pipe is

$${x \over 3}L\sqrt {{{{\rho _1}} \over {{\rho _2}}}} $$ ..... (ii)

Comparing Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$x = 4$$

questions.questionText: An observer is riding on a bicycle and moving towards a hill at $$18 \,\mathrm{kmh}^{-1}$$. He hears a sound from a source at some distance behind him directly as well as after its reflection from the hill. If the original frequency of the sound as emitted by source is $$640 \mathrm{~Hz}$$ and velocity of the sound in air is $$320 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$$, the beat frequency between the two sounds heard by observer will be _____________ $$\mathrm{Hz}$$.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
$${f_1} = {f_0}\left( {{{320 - 5} \over {320}}} \right) = 640\left( {{{315} \over {320}}} \right)$$

$$ = 630$$ Hz

$${f_3} = {f_0}$$ [No relative motion]

$${f_2} = {f_0}\left[ {{{320 + 5} \over {320}}} \right] = 640\left( {{{325} \over {320}}} \right)$$

$$ = 650$$

Beat frequency $$ = {f_2} - {f_1}$$

$$ = 650 - 630 = 20$$ Hz

questions.questionText:
A transverse wave on a string is described by $y=3 \sin (36 t+0.018 x+\pi / 4)$. where $x, y$ are in cm and $t$ in seconds. The least distance between the two successive crests in the wave is $\_\_\_\_$ cm . (Nearest integer)

$$ (\pi=3.14) $$
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
The standard equation for a traveling wave is $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{A} \sin (\omega \mathrm{t}+\mathrm{kx}+\phi)$, where $\omega$ is the angular frequency, k is the wave number and $\phi$ is the phase constant.

By comparing this with the given equation $\mathrm{y}=3 \sin \left(36 \mathrm{t}+0.018 \mathrm{x}+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$, the wave number is $\mathrm{k}= 0.018 \mathrm{~cm}^{-1}$.

The distance between two successive crests (or any two successive points in the same phase) is equal to wavelength ( $\lambda$ ) of the wave.

The wave number k is related to the wavelength by the formula:

$$ k=\frac{2 \pi}{\lambda} $$

Putting the value $\mathrm{k}=0.018$ and $\pi=3.14$ into the formula :

$$ 0.018=\frac{2 \times 3.14}{\lambda} $$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $\lambda=\frac{6.28}{0.018}$

$$\Rightarrow $$ $$ \lambda \approx 349 \mathrm{~cm} $$

Therefore, the wavelength of transverse wave is 349 cm .

Thus, the correct answer is 349 .

questions.questionText: A pipe of length $$85$$ $$cm$$ is closed from one end. Find the number of possible natural oscillations of air column in the pipe whose frequencies lie below $$1250$$ $$Hz$$. The velocity of sound in air is $$340$$ $$m/s$$.
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Length of pipe $$=85$$ $$cm$$ $$=0.85m$$

Pipe is closed from one end so it behaves as a closed organ pipe

Frequency of oscillations of air column in closed organ pipe is given by,

$$f = {{\left( {2n - 1} \right)\upsilon } \over {4L}}$$

$$f = {{\left( {2n - 1} \right)\upsilon } \over {4L}} \le 1250$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{\left( {2n - 1} \right) \times 340} \over {0.85 \times 4}} \le 1250$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2n - 1 \le 12.5 \approx 6$$

Possible value of n = 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6

So, number of possible natural frequencies lie below 1250 Hz is 6.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Sound level = 10$${\log _{10}}\left( {{I \over {{I_0}}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ 120 = 10$${\log _{10}}\left( {{I \over {{{10}^{ - 12}}}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ 12 = $${\log _{10}}\left( {{I \over {{{10}^{ - 12}}}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ 1012 = $${{I \over {{{10}^{ - 12}}}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ I = 1 W/m2

Also we know,

I = $${P \over {4\pi {r^2}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ 1 = $${2 \over {4\pi {r^2}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ r = 40 cm

questions.questionText:
Assume that the displacement(s) of air is
proportional to the pressure difference ($$\Delta $$p)
created by a sound wave. Displacement (s)
further depends on the speed of sound (v),
density of air ($$\rho $$) and the frequency (f). If
$$\Delta $$p ~ 10 Pa, v ~ 300 m/s, $$\rho $$ ~ 1 kg/m<sup>3</sup> and f ~ 1000 Hz,
then s will be of the order of (take the
multiplicative constant to be 1) :
questions.subject: Physics
questions.solution:
Given, S $$ \propto $$ $$\Delta $$p

and Proportionally constant = 1

We know,

$$\Delta $$p = S$$\beta $$k

= $$\rho $$v2 $$ \times $$ $${\omega \over v} \times S$$

= $${\rho v\omega S}$$

$$ \therefore $$ S = $${{\Delta p} \over {\rho v\omega }}$$

= $${{\Delta p} \over {\rho v2\pi f}}$$

= $${{\Delta p} \over {\rho vf}}$$

[As Proportionally constant = 1 so assume 2$$\pi $$ = 1]

$$ = {{10} \over {1 \times 300 \times 1000}}$$

$$ = {1 \over {30}}mm$$

$$ \approx {3 \over {100}}mm$$

questions.questionText: The mass per unit length of a uniform wire is 0.135 g/cm. A transverse wave of the form y = $$-$$ 0.21 sin (x + 30t) is produced in it, where x is in meter and t is in second. A stationary tuning fork is in resonance with an air column in a pipe. If the tuning fork is moved with

questions.questionText: A set of 20 tuning forks is arranged in a series of increasing frequencies. If each fork gives 4 beats with respect to the preceding fork and the frequency of the last fork is twice the frequency of the first, then the frequency of last fork is _________ Hz.

questions.questionText: When a car is approaching the observer, the frequency of horn is $$100 \mathrm{~Hz}$$. After passing the observer, it is $$50 \mathrm{~Hz}$$. If the observer moves with the car, the frequency will be $$\frac{x}{3} \mathrm{~Hz}$$ where $$x=$$ ________________.
questions.subject: Physics
